root_cause: Replica count is oscillating for the workload instead of converging
evidence: ReplicaSet 'api-6dbf78c7f9' scaled as: up to 6 -> down to 4 -> up to 6 -> down to 4
Scaling direction reversed 3 times over 17.0 minutes
The same ReplicaSet kept changing size instead of a clean one-way rollout handoff
ReplicaSet 'api-6dbf78c7f9' still has only 2/4 available replicas
HPA 'api-hpa' also rescaled the workload back and forth as: 6 -> 4 -> 6 -> 4

causes.message: Timeline shows repeated scale reversals for ReplicaSet 'api-6dbf78c7f9'
Replica count keeps alternating between scale-up and scale-down instead of settling on a stable desired size
Controllers or autoscalers are repeatedly reversing replica decisions for the same workload
ReplicaSet remains below stable capacity (2/4 available) while desired replica counts keep reversing

pod:api-6dbf78c7f9-zr8kd: Pod belongs to a workload whose desired replica count kept reversing

likely_causes: An HPA or other autoscaler is responding too aggressively and repeatedly reversing desired replica counts
Manual or GitOps updates to spec.replicas are fighting with autoscaler-driven changes
Conflicting controller inputs are alternately scaling the same workload up and down before it stabilizes
Replica health never stabilizes long enough for the workload to converge on one desired size
